items.title: Ripple Launches Treasury Management System with Native Digital Asset Capabilities
items.summary: The novel platform allows CFOs and their treasury teams to manage fiat and digital assets in a single system, Ripple said.

items.title: Franklin Templeton launches crypto division with 250 Digital acquisition
items.summary: The asset manager is creating a new “Franklin Crypto” unit to expand beyond ETFs and target institutional demand for active digital asset strategies.

items.summary: Hedera (HBAR), up 3.6% from Tuesday, was also among the top performers.

items.title: Hong Kong misses March target for first stablecoin licences
items.summary: Hong Kong’s first stablecoin licences failed to materialize by the expected end of March target, with the HKMA saying only that it is still advancing the process.

items.title: Bitcoin’s crashes are shrinking, and Wall Street is starting to notice
items.summary: Not all analysts agree that further drawdowns are over, as Bloomberg Analyst Mike McGlone insists the crypto bubble is over and bitcoin could still revisit $10,000.

items.title: Who is Kevin Warsh? Trump’s Fed pick wants ‘regime change’ at central bank
items.summary: US President Donald Trump nominated Kevin Warsh to lead the Fed, but the financier’s plans to lower interest rates may come up against hard economic realities and a split board.

items.title: Gen Z turns Bitcoin into a solid portfolio diversifier
items.summary: Gen Z embraces Bitcoin despite acknowledged volatility. Younger investors treat crypto risk as portfolio diversification rather than pure speculation.

items.summary: The funding round, anchored by Amazon, Nvidia, and SoftBank, is the largest private funding in history.

items.summary: Experts cite portfolio rebalancing and geopolitical easing as driving the crypto market rally, though caution persists.

items.title: Bitcoin snaps 5-month losing streak: Key BTC price levels to watch in April

items.title: Grayscale’s research head says tokenization will happen in waves and explains how to play it
items.summary: Investors looking to bet on tokenization should think in phases, with institution-friendly networks like Canton likely winning first and Avalanche, Ethereum capturing more upside later, Grayscale's Zach Pandl said.

items.title: Brazil's B3 exchange to offer bitcoin-linked 'event contracts' for the ultra-rich
items.summary: The contracts are regulated by Brazil's securities regulator and designed for professional investors with at least 10 million reais ($1.9 million) in assets.
